proved: The public syzygy:research/plugin@1.0.0 WIT source declares one world and no host imports.
Pinned Bytecode Alliance wit-parser 0.223.1 parses and resolves that package as one zero-import world with one exported interface.
The invocation envelope permits only version, plugin identity, contribution identity, and an optional bounded project/source snapshot.
Unknown ambient fields, malformed identity, duplicate sources, cyclic serialization, and oversized project/source/envelope content fail closed.
Output is either a bounded non-empty no-change reason or one to 32 existing schema-validated revision-guarded proposals.
Direct-mutation markers, unknown output kinds/fields, empty proposal batches, and malformed proposals fail closed.
The existing manifest schema remains compatible; the example opts into the versioned reference world without claiming its marker artifact is executable.
MCP embeds the exact WIT source and world identifier while reporting published-zero-imports-no-runtime.
The structural audit locks parser use, zero imports, bounded envelopes, proposal validation, and truthful runtime status.
notProved: The example marker is a valid WebAssembly component or implements the published world.
Any component is discovered, installed, parsed as a binary, instantiated, or executed.
A runtime enforces memory, fuel, wall-time, stdout/stderr, trap, cancellation, or output-flood limits.
A runtime rejects unexpected component imports or denies filesystem, network, environment, clock, randomness, Drive, or model access.
A plugin invocation is connected to the authority broker or product UI.
Native MCP plugin lifecycle, signing, upgrades, marketplace behavior, or third-party plugin usefulness.
